name: Two Pointers
description: Use two indices moving through a sequence to find pairs, remove elements, or partition.

name: Sliding Window
description: Maintain a dynamic subarray/substring window with add/remove invariants.

name: Binary Search
description: Halve the search space on sorted data or monotonic answer functions.

name: Prefix Sum
description: Precompute cumulative sums for O(1) range queries.

name: Hash Map
description: Trade space for speed with key-value lookups and frequency counting.

name: Stack / Monotonic Stack
description: Track candidates with LIFO order or monotonic properties.

name: Queue / BFS
description: Explore level by level for shortest paths and multi-source floods.

name: DFS / Backtracking
description: Recursive exploration with undo for search spaces.

name: Dynamic Programming
description: Define states and transitions; memoize or tabulate.

name: Greedy
description: Sort + choose locally best option with proof of correctness.

name: Union Find
description: Disjoint sets for connectivity and component merging.

name: Trie
description: Prefix trees for dictionary and string prefix queries.

name: Heap / Priority Queue
description: Always extract min/max in logarithmic time.

name: Bit Manipulation
description: Use bits as compact sets or arithmetic shortcuts.

name: Linked List Pointers
description: Dummy heads, fast/slow pointers, and in-place rewiring.

name: Tree Traversal
description: Preorder, inorder, postorder, and level-order walks.

name: Graph Algorithms
description: DFS/BFS, topological sort, Dijkstra, and MST ideas.

name: Intervals
description: Sort and merge, sweep line, and meeting-room style logic.

name: Kadane's Algorithm
description: Maximum subarray via running local optima.

name: Topological Sort
description: Order nodes in a DAG for course-schedule style problems.
